title: Static template catalog for demo (Railway-style one-click deploys)
description:
## Context

For the demo and production use, we need two distinct addon-related surfaces:

1. **User flow** — project members deploy addon instances (postgres, redis, etc.) into their projects from a Railway-style catalog picker.
2. **Admin flow** — platform admins manage the addon catalog (create definitions, update chart versions, deactivate entries, add cluster-scoped overrides).

The catalog is seeded with 6 global definitions (postgres, redis, mongodb, kafka, mysql, rabbitmq) but admins need a UI to manage them over time.

---

## User flow: Deploy an addon instance

### Page: /projects/[id]/addons (list + launch)

- Shows all running addon instances for the project
- "Add Addon" button opens the catalog picker

### Catalog picker (modal or /projects/[id]/addons/new)

- Fetches GET /v1/addon-definitions (pass ?cluster_id= for cluster-specific overrides)
- Displays cards: icon + name + description + tags
- User clicks a card, enters an instance name, optionally overrides values
- Submits POST /v1/addon-instances { project_id, name, definition_id }
- Redirects to addon instance detail, phase starts as Pending

### Custom deploy (advanced)

- "Custom" option in picker: user supplies type + chart { repo, name, version } + values
- Submits POST /v1/addon-instances { project_id, name, type, chart, values } (no definition_id)

### Addon instance detail: /projects/[id]/addons/[instance_id]

- Shows phase badge (Pending / Deploying / Running / Degraded / Failed)
- Shows exports (connection strings) once Running
- Delete button → DELETE /v1/addon-instances/{id}

---

## Admin flow: Manage addon catalog

### Page: /admin/addon-definitions (list)

- Fetches GET /v1/addon-definitions?is_active=true (and optionally false to show inactive)
- Table: name, slug, type, chart version, cluster scope, active toggle
- "New Definition" button
- Row actions: Edit, Deactivate

### Page: /admin/addon-definitions/new

- Form: name, slug, type, icon, description, tags, cluster_id (optional), chart config (repo/name/version), default_values (YAML/JSON editor)
- Submits POST /v1/addon-definitions

### Page: /admin/addon-definitions/[id]/edit

- Pre-filled form from GET /v1/addon-definitions/{id}
- Submits PATCH /v1/addon-definitions/{id}
- Supports updating chart version (common operation when chart releases new version)

### Deactivate

- Toggle or button → DELETE /v1/addon-definitions/{id} (soft-delete, sets is_active=false)
- Existing instances are unaffected (definition_id FK is nullable, SET NULL on delete)

---

## API reference

### Catalog (admin-managed)
```
GET    /v1/addon-definitions              ?cluster_id=&type=&is_active=true
POST   /v1/addon-definitions              { cluster_id?, name, slug, type, icon, tags, chart_config, default_values }
GET    /v1/addon-definitions/{id}
PATCH  /v1/addon-definitions/{id}         partial update
DELETE /v1/addon-definitions/{id}         soft-delete (is_active=false)
```

### Instances (user-facing)
```
POST   /v1/addon-instances               { project_id, name, definition_id } or { project_id, name, type, chart, values }
GET    /v1/addon-instances?project_id=X  list for a project
GET    /v1/addon-instances/{id}
DELETE /v1/addon-instances/{id}
```

### AddonDefinitionRead shape
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "cluster_id": null,
  "name": "PostgreSQL",
  "slug": "postgres",
  "description": "Open-source relational database",
  "icon": "🐘",
  "tags": ["database", "popular"],
  "type": "postgres",
  "chart_config": { "repo": "...", "name": "postgresql", "version": "12.1.2" },
  "default_values": null,
  "is_active": true
}
```

### AddonInstanceRead shape
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "definition_id": "uuid or null",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "name": "postgres-main",
  "type": "postgres",
  "chart_config": { ... },
  "phase": "Running",
  "exports": { "host": "postgres.svc", "port": "5432" },
  "deployed_at": "2026-02-19T..."
}
```

---

## Notes

- The 6 global seed definitions cover the common cases — admins only need the UI for adding new types or updating chart versions.
- Cluster-scoped definitions (cluster_id set) allow per-cluster overrides, e.g. HA Postgres only for production clusters. The admin form should support selecting a cluster.
- Chart refs in seed data use Bitnami placeholders — see kubenest-backend-a0z for the migration to official charts.
- Phase updates arrive via SSE (kubenest-ui-9ch) — the instance detail page should subscribe to addon_status_update events.
notes:
## Backend API — UPDATED (addon definition/instance split, 2026-02-19)

The addon backend was refactored into a two-layer architecture. All frontend addon code must use the new endpoints.

### Catalog API — /v1/addon-definitions
```
GET  /v1/addon-definitions                     → AddonDefinitionList
     ?cluster_id=&type=&is_active=true
GET  /v1/addon-definitions/{id}                → AddonDefinitionRead
POST /v1/addon-definitions                     → create (admin only)
PATCH /v1/addon-definitions/{id}               → update
DELETE /v1/addon-definitions/{id}              → soft-delete (is_active=false)
```

AddonDefinitionRead shape:
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "cluster_id": null,
  "name": "PostgreSQL",
  "slug": "postgres",
  "description": "Open-source relational database",
  "icon": "🐘",
  "tags": ["database", "popular"],
  "type": "postgres",
  "chart_config": { "repo": "...", "name": "postgresql", "version": "12.1.2" },
  "default_values": null,
  "is_active": true
}
```

Global definitions (cluster_id=null) are always returned. Pass ?cluster_id=X to also get cluster-specific overrides.
6 standard addons are seeded: postgres, redis, mongodb, kafka, mysql, rabbitmq.

### Instance API — /v1/addon-instances
```
POST   /v1/addon-instances                     → deploy → 201
GET    /v1/addon-instances?project_id=X        → list (project_id required)
GET    /v1/addon-instances/{id}                → detail
DELETE /v1/addon-instances/{id}                → uninstall → 204
```

AddonInstanceCreate (from catalog definition):
```json
{
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "name": "postgres-main",
  "definition_id": "uuid"
}
```

AddonInstanceCreate (custom, no definition):
```json
{
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "name": "my-redis",
  "type": "redis",
  "chart": { "repo": "...", "name": "redis", "version": "17.0.0" },
  "values": {}
}
```

AddonInstanceRead shape (same phase/exports fields as before):
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "definition_id": "uuid or null",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "name": "postgres-main",
  "type": "postgres",
  "chart_config": { ... },
  "phase": "Pending",
  "exports": null,
  "deployed_at": null
}
```

### What changes in the frontend

1. Template catalog page: can now be API-driven — fetch GET /v1/addon-definitions instead of using a static TEMPLATES constant. Still fine to keep TEMPLATES as a fallback/loading state.

2. Deploy flow from catalog: pass definition_id in the AddonInstanceCreate body (no need to supply chart manually — backend copies it from the definition).

3. Addon list page: change GET /v1/addons?project_id=X → GET /v1/addon-instances?project_id=X

4. Addon deploy: change POST /v1/addons → POST /v1/addon-instances

5. Addon delete: change DELETE /v1/addons/{id} → DELETE /v1/addon-instances/{id}

6. URL route rename: /projects/[id]/addons → /projects/[id]/addon-instances (or keep UI path the same, just fix the API calls)

### Old API (deleted — do not use)
- POST /v1/addons
- GET /v1/addons
- GET /v1/addons/{id}
- DELETE /v1/addons/{id}

### op3 intent handler ✅
addon_install and addon_uninstall are handled — same as before, no change from frontend perspective.

### Chart refs
Bitnami charts are placeholders in seed data. See kubenest-backend-a0z for replacement task. Deploy flow is fully wired.

title: Complete workload deployment flow
description: Deploy workloads, view workload details with real-time SSE status updates

title: IdP config UI + SSO login flow
description: Admin panel: configure OIDC/SAML IdP (provider type, client ID, client secret, discovery URL). Test connection button. SSO login page (redirect to IdP instead of username/password form when SSO configured). Group → role mapping table UI. SCIM token generation for HR system integration.

title: Teleport web terminal + JIT access request UI
description: Web terminal embedded in workload detail page (Teleport app service iframe or native xterm.js). Connect button launches short-lived cert session. JIT access request flow: request prod access → notify approvers → Slack/email → approve/deny → cert issued. Session recording viewer: playback of past kubectl sessions.

title: Audit log viewer + compliance report download
description: Audit log page: table with actor, action, resource, timestamp, IP. Filters: date range, actor, resource type, action. Click row for full detail. Export button: CSV or JSON. Compliance report page: select report type (SOC2, ISO27001), date range → generate PDF evidence pack. Download button.

title: Provisioning progress and logs view
description: Real-time provisioning status page for a cluster being provisioned. Shows: current stage (Provisioning VMs → Installing k3s → Bootstrapping → Ready), progress percentage, elapsed time, live log tail from provisioning job. Polls GET /api/v1/provisioning-jobs/{id} or uses WebSocket for updates. Error state with retry option. Route: /clusters/{id}/provisioning.

title: Add Helm chart deploy mode to workload creation form
description: ## Context The backend now supports deploying workloads via custom Helm charts instead of the default kubenest-workload chart. The API accepts two new optional fields on POST /workloads: - `chart`: `{ repo: string, name: string, version: string }` - `values`: arbitrary JSON object of Helm values The workload response object now includes `chart_config: { chart?: ChartSpec, values?: Record } | null`. ## Goal Extend the "Deploy New Workload" form and workload detail page to support Helm chart deployments alongside the existing image-based flow. --- ## Files to change ### 1. `types/api.ts` Add `ChartSpec` interface and extend existing types: \`\`\`ts export interface ChartSpec { repo: string; // Helm repo URL, e.g. https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami name: string; // Chart name, e.g. postgresql version: string; // Semver chart version, e.g. 12.1.2 } \`\`\` Add to `Workload` interface: \`\`\`ts chart_config: { chart?: ChartSpec; values?: Record } | null; \`\`\` Add to `CreateWorkloadRequest`: \`\`\`ts chart?: ChartSpec; values?: Record; \`\`\` --- ### 2. `app/projects/[id]/workloads/new/page.tsx` #### Deploy mode toggle Add a mode selector at the top of the form with two options: **Image** (default) and **Helm Chart**. Use `useState<'image' | 'chart'>('image')`. #### Updated Zod schema Remove the hard `z.string().min(1)` requirement on `image`. Add chart fields. Use `.superRefine()` to cross-validate: - In `image` mode: `image` is required - In `chart` mode: `chart.repo`, `chart.name`, `chart.version` are all required; `image` is ignored \`\`\`ts const chartSpecSchema = z.object({ repo: z.string().url('Must be a valid URL'), name: z.string().min(1, 'Chart name is required'), version: z.string().min(1, 'Version is required'), }); const workloadSchema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(1).regex(/^[a-z0-9-]+$/), image: z.string().optional(), replicas: z.number().int().min(0).max(100), port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional().nullable(), ingress: ingressSchema.optional(), chart: chartSpecSchema.optional(), valuesYaml: z.string().optional(), // raw YAML/JSON text input, parsed before submit }); \`\`\` #### Conditional field rendering When mode is `'image'`: show `Container Image` field (required), hide chart fields. When mode is `'chart'`: hide image field, show three chart fields (Repo, Name, Version) + optional Helm Values textarea. #### Helm Values textarea - Collapsible section (same pattern as Ingress) - Accepts YAML or JSON (use `js-yaml` if already in deps, else JSON only is fine) - Parse to object before submitting; show parse error inline if invalid #### `onSubmit` handler changes \`\`\`ts // image mode (current behavior unchanged) if (mode === 'image') { payload.image = data.image; } // chart mode if (mode === 'chart') { payload.chart = data.chart; if (data.valuesYaml) { try { payload.values = JSON.parse(data.valuesYaml); // or yaml.load() } catch { setError('valuesYaml', { message: 'Invalid JSON/YAML' }); return; } } } \`\`\` --- ### 3. `app/workloads/[id]/page.tsx` In the **Workload Details** card, update the source row: - If `workload.chart_config?.chart` exists: show chart badge with repo/name@version instead of the Image row - If `workload.image` exists: show the existing Image row (no change) - Both can coexist (image + values override case), but chart takes priority as the label Replace the hardcoded `Image` entry in the details array with: \`\`\`tsx workload.chart_config?.chart ? { label: 'Helm Chart', value: ( {workload.chart_config.chart.name}@{workload.chart_config.chart.version} ({workload.chart_config.chart.repo}) ), } : { label: 'Image', value: ( {workload.image} ), }, \`\`\` If `chart_config.values` is non-empty, add a collapsible **Helm Values** section below the details card showing the values as formatted JSON. --- ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] Deploy form shows Image/Helm Chart toggle - [ ] Image mode: existing behaviour unchanged, all existing tests pass - [ ] Chart mode: chart repo/name/version fields shown and validated - [ ] Chart mode: Helm Values textarea accepts JSON and shows parse errors - [ ] Submitting in chart mode sends `{ chart: {...}, values: {...} }` in the API payload (no `image`) - [ ] Workload detail page shows "Helm Chart" source row instead of "Image" when `chart_config` is set - [ ] TypeScript types are updated — no `any` casts needed ## Notes - `WorkloadPhase` in `types/api.ts` currently has lowercase values (`running`, `failed`, etc.) but the backend returns Title-case (`Running`, `Failed`). Check if this mismatch causes any issues while you are in this file. - No new dependencies are strictly required; use JSON.parse for values unless js-yaml is already installed. - The `lib/api/workloads.ts` and `hooks/useWorkloads.ts` files do NOT need changes — they pass the payload through as-is.
close_reason: Implemented: Image/Helm Chart toggle on workload form, ChartSpec types, chart_config on workload detail page with collapsible Helm Values section. All typechecks pass.

title: Workload detail page refresh (canine.sh-inspired layout)
description:
Narrow refresh of /workloads/[id] only. Stack-deploy is a separate future epic (Apr 14 design doc's stack-deploy sections are obsolete).

Based on docs/design/workload-stackdeploy-rehaul.md (Apr 14) reconciled with 2026-04-15 decisions.

### Header
- Name + favorite star + status badge
- Primary domain link (external)
- Org/project/cluster/namespace chips
- Restart button (disabled, beaded for later)
- Deploy split button (redeploy = re-run helm upgrade with current values)
- Inline-editable strip under the header for image tag, replicas, port — click to edit in place. Each save calls PATCH, records a deployment row, triggers a helm upgrade.

### Tabs (6)
1. Deployments — history table + rollback (built)
2. Environment — env vars + secrets
3. Domains — add/remove domains, TLS toggle
4. Logs — real kubectl logs streaming via hub (needs backend endpoint bead)
5. Metrics — empty state, Grafana panels embed when monitoring lands
6. Settings — delete with confirmation (rename/transfer later)

### Dropped from original plan
- Services tab: no cron/worker sub-workloads yet — bring back when it has content
- Shell tab (proposed 2026-04-15 then dropped): belongs in kn CLI (kubenest-backend-88a.3), not UI v1

Out of scope: sidebar, dashboard, apps page, theme change (stays light).

title: Workload page: tabbed shell + header + inline-edit strip
description: Rebuild /workloads/[id] page.tsx. New header: breadcrumb, name + favorite star + status badge, primary domain link, org/project/cluster/namespace chips, Restart (disabled) + Deploy split button. Under the header, a compact editable strip showing image tag, replicas, port — each click-to-edit with save/cancel that PATCHes the workload. Below that, a 6-tab strip rendering empty states. Follow-up sub-beads fill each tab. Use the existing shadcn tabs component.

title: Workload page: Deployments tab content
description: Move the existing DeploymentHistoryCard into the Deployments tab. Make it the default tab. No new functionality; pure relocation from the current long-scroll layout. Blocked by the tab shell bead.

title: Workload page: Domains tab
description: Rename Ingress → Domains (matches Apr 14 design doc). Lists current domains with external-link icons, per-row delete, and an add-domain form. Shows TLS state per row (Lets Encrypt toggle — see aqf). Each mutation records a deployment row. Blocked by tab shell bead.

title: Workload page: Environment tab content
description: Move env vars display + WorkloadSecretsCard into the Environment tab. Consider consolidating into a single editable table with a column indicating secret vs plain. Blocked by the tab shell bead.

title: Workload page: Settings tab (delete + danger zone)
description: Settings tab is delete-only for v1 per Apr 14 design doc. Danger zone card with delete confirmation. Rename and transfer owner are later beads. Inline edits for image/replicas/port live in the HEADER STRIP (see .1), not here. Once the header strip is inline-editable, remove the old WorkloadEditDialog modal entirely. Blocked by tab shell bead.

title: Workload page: Metrics tab stub (Grafana placeholder)
description: Stubbed Metrics tab that says 'Metrics will show embedded Grafana panels once the monitoring stack is wired up'. No dummy charts. Should render an empty state pointing at the future Grafana integration bead. Blocked by the tab shell bead.

title: Workload page: Logs tab (real kubectl logs streaming)
description: Real log streaming for the workload's pods. UI tab with a terminal-style viewer, pod selector dropdown (one per replica), follow/pause toggle, download, level filter. Depends on a backend streaming endpoint that tails logs from the tenant cluster via the hub — file that as a separate backend bead in kubenest-backend and block this bead on it. No dummy logs. Blocked by the tab shell bead and the backend log endpoint bead.

title: Workload page: Shell tab (kubectl exec in browser)
description: Browser-based kubectl exec into a pod, xterm.js with a websocket pty. Pod selector. Depends on a backend websocket endpoint that proxies to the tenant cluster via the hub — file as a separate backend bead and block this bead on it. Blocked by the tab shell bead and the backend exec endpoint bead.
close_reason: Shell tab deferred — belongs in kn CLI (kubenest-backend-88a.3), not the UI v1

title: Delete app/demo-workloads route
description: Once the real /workloads/[id] refresh has shipped, delete app/demo-workloads/[id]/page.tsx and any references. It was a Mar 24 demo stub with dummy logs/metrics and is obsolete.

title: Policy management UI: create policies, view violations, visual editor
description: Policy list page: all DeploymentPolicies with status (active/inactive), scope (cluster/project). Create/edit policy form: rule type selector (registry allowlist, image signing, resource limits, etc), environment scope (staging/prod). Policy violation detail: which deploy was blocked, which rule failed, suggested fix. Built-in policy library with one-click enable.

title: Approval workflow UI: request, review, approve/reject deploys
description: Pending approvals badge in nav (red count). Approvals inbox page: list of pending deploys awaiting review. Deploy detail: diff of what's changing, requester, target environment. Approve/reject with comment. Requester view: deploy status shows 'Awaiting approval from X'. Notification preferences (email, Slack).

title: Workload metrics: real-time CPU/memory/request charts in workload detail
description:
## Context

When kube-prometheus-stack is installed as a cluster addon (optional), Prometheus scrapes workload metrics and Grafana provides dashboards. The workload detail page should surface basic metrics inline without requiring users to open Grafana directly.

## What to display

Basic real-time metrics for the workload (last 30 minutes, auto-refresh every 15s):
- CPU usage (millicores)
- Memory usage (MiB)
- HTTP request rate (req/s) — only if the workload exposes metrics endpoint
- Error rate — optional

These are fetched directly from the Prometheus API (not Grafana embed) so we control the rendering.

## How it works

Cluster model (backend) will have a prometheus_url field when monitoring is installed.
The backend exposes a proxy/passthrough endpoint:
  GET /workloads/:id/metrics?query=<promql>&start=&end=&step=

(Proxying avoids CORS issues and keeps Prometheus internal to the cluster.)

Alternatively, if Grafana is exposed, embed a pre-built dashboard panel via iframe.

## UI changes (app/workloads/[id]/page.tsx)

Add a **Metrics** card below the Scale Controls card. Shown only when:
- `workload.phase === 'Running'` (or 'Degraded')
- `cluster.prometheus_url` is set (need to fetch cluster via project)

Inside the card:
- Three small sparkline charts (Recharts or similar, already check if in deps)
- Time range selector: 15m | 1h | 6h
- Auto-refresh toggle (on by default, 15s interval using useInterval)
- Loading skeleton while fetching

## API client changes (lib/api/)

New file: `lib/api/metrics.ts`
```ts
export const metricsApi = {
  query: (workloadId: string, params: MetricsQueryParams) =>
    apiClient.get<MetricsResponse>(`/workloads/${workloadId}/metrics`, { params }),
};
```

## Hook (hooks/useWorkloadMetrics.ts)

```ts
export function useWorkloadMetrics(workloadId: string, range: '15m' | '1h' | '6h') {
  return useQuery({
    queryKey: ['metrics', workloadId, range],
    queryFn: () => metricsApi.query(workloadId, { range }),
    refetchInterval: 15_000,
    enabled: !!workloadId,
  });
}
```

## Backend prerequisite

A GET /workloads/:id/metrics endpoint that queries Prometheus and returns:
```json
{
  "cpu": [{ "t": 1234567890, "v": 125.4 }, ...],
  "memory": [{ "t": 1234567890, "v": 256.0 }, ...],
  "rps": [{ "t": 1234567890, "v": 12.3 }, ...]
}
```
This backend endpoint is a separate bead. The frontend can stub it initially with mock data.

## Notes

- Check if Recharts or similar is already in package.json before adding a dependency.
- Metrics card should gracefully degrade: if cluster has no prometheus_url, show a subtle
  "Install the monitoring addon to view metrics" message instead of the charts.
- Do NOT embed Grafana iframes — too heavy, auth is complex, poor UX.
- The monitoring addon (kube-prometheus-stack) is optional at the cluster level.
  Backend bead: "Add prometheus_url to Cluster and expose /workloads/:id/metrics proxy"

title: Env vars + secrets: unified GitHub-Actions-style list
description: Replace the split Environment-variables / Secrets cards with a single unified list. Each row: key field, value field inline, a lock-toggle icon marking it as a secret (masks the value input, stores via the secrets API). Plain rows store via workload.env PATCH. Add row via a '+ Add variable' button. Delete per-row. Match GitHub Actions variables/secrets UX. Handles the redacted-echo-back gotcha: redacted values are read-only until explicitly replaced.

title: Epic: Workload & StackDeploy detail page rehaul (UI)
description: Canine-style workload detail page and Railway-style stack-deploy create/detail pages. See ~/sb/kubenest-backend/docs/design/workload-stackdeploy-rehaul.md. Workload first, stack-deploy second.

title: Shared: DeploymentsHistory table component
description: Reusable table: commit/description, sha (linked), status badge, deployed-at (relative), user, Logs button. Used by both workload and stack-deploy detail pages. Pulls from GET /{target}/{id}/deployments.

title: StackDeploy detail page: tabbed layout
description: Tabs: Deployments, Environment, Metrics (placeholder), Settings. Top bar mirrors workload. Deploy button triggers POST /stack-deploys/{id}/redeploy. Treat as monolith — no per-component ops.

title: StackDeploy: Environment tab
description: Stack-level env vars exposed by composite chart values.

title: StackDeploy: Settings tab (delete)
description: Reuses shared delete confirmation modal.

title: Shared: delete confirmation modal
description: Reused by workload Settings and stack-deploy Settings tabs.

title: Workload detail page: tabbed layout skeleton
description: Tabs: Deployments, Services (empty-state stub), Environment, Domains, Metrics (placeholder), Settings. Top bar: name, star, status badge, primary domain with external link, breadcrumb chips (org/project/cluster/workload), Restart (disabled), Deploy button. Deploy triggers POST /workloads/{id}/redeploy.

title: Workload: inline field edits (image tag, replicas, port)
description: Edit-in-place on the detail page. Each submit calls PATCH /workloads/{id} and refreshes deployment history.

title: Workload: Environment tab (env vars + secrets CRUD)
description: Add/edit/delete env vars and secrets. Masked display for secrets. Batched save.

title: Workload: Domains tab
description: Add/remove domains with inline validation.

title: Workload: Metrics tab placeholder
description: Empty state with 'Metrics coming soon' message. Grafana embed lands in a later bead.

title: Workload: Settings tab (delete app)
description: Danger zone with delete button + confirmation modal.

title: StackDeploy create page: per-component cards with staged save
description: Railway-style. Vertical list of component cards (icon, name, repo/image, volume, env vars, Save Config). Staged in client state. Bottom Deploy button disabled until all saved.

title: Traffic management UI: canary, A/B, blue-green controls
description: Traffic split UI on workload detail page (only shown when service mesh enabled). Canary: slider for % traffic to new version (0-100%). A/B: header rule builder (header name + value → target version). Blue-green: instant cutover button with confirmation. Live traffic chart showing actual split. Rollback button to 100% stable.

title: Service topology map + distributed tracing viewer
description: Interactive service topology graph (D3 or similar): nodes = workloads, edges = actual traffic (from mesh metrics). Edge labels show RPS, error rate, P99 latency. Click edge to see traces. Distributed tracing viewer: trace waterfall diagram from Tempo/Jaeger. Filter by service, time range, error status.

title: Add real-time SSE status updates
description: Implement SSE provider and hooks for live workload status updates

title: App-first create: /apps/new staged-save form
description:
Build the /apps/new page — the primary create flow for KubeNest. An App is a StackDeploy with inline components. The form buffers all components client-side until the user clicks Deploy, then POSTs to /api/v1/apps.

### Layout (Canine-inspired, single page)
- Name field + project selector at top
- Component list: cards for each workload/addon, + Add Component button
- Deploy button at bottom (disabled until at least one component)
- No wizard steps — single scroll, progressive disclosure

### Component types
- Workload: web service (deployment+ingress), worker (deployment, no port), job, cronjob (future)
- Addon: pick from addon catalog (AddonDefinition), shows export_schema keys

### Env var wiring
- Each workload component has an env var section
- 'Insert from component' picker: lists addon components in the buffer, shows their export_schema keys
- Selection creates export_ref: {component: 'name', export_key: 'KEY'}
- depends_on auto-inferred from export_refs

### Backend
- POST /api/v1/apps (already shipped, kubenest-backend-5d8.2)
- GET /api/v1/addon-definitions for catalog
- AddonDefinition.export_schema for wiring picker (already shipped, 5d8.1)

### Not in scope
- Template-based deploy (separate flow via /settings/stack-templates)
- Build pipeline (Option 2, kubenest-ui-mpu)
- Jobs/CronJobs component type (future)

title: App create: form shell with name, project, component list, Deploy
description:
Scaffold /apps/new page.tsx. Canine-style FormRow layout. Fields:
- App name (slug, required)
- Project selector (dropdown from GET /api/v1/projects)
- Empty component list with '+ Add Component' button (placeholder)
- Deploy button (disabled until name + project + ≥1 component)
- Cancel button → /apps

Client-side state: useReducer or zustand store holding the component buffer. No API calls on save — everything buffered until Deploy click.

Reuse FormRow, OptionCard, SectionDivider from the workload create page (kubenest-ui-clv). Consider extracting them to components/ui/ if not already.

title: App create: workload component card
description:
When user clicks '+ Add Component → Web Service / Worker', add a workload card to the buffer. Card contents:
- Component name (editable slug, auto-generated default like 'web' or 'worker-1')
- Source toggle: Container Image / Git Repository (reuse from clv)
- Image + tag fields (image source)
- Git repo + branch + build method (git source)
- Port field (hidden for Worker type)
- Replicas
- Env vars section (textarea for now, wiring picker in separate bead)
- Remove component button (X)

Card is collapsible — shows name + type + image summary when collapsed.

Workload type mapping:
- Web Service → type: 'workload', workload_spec with port + ingress
- Worker → type: 'workload', workload_spec without port/ingress

title: App create: addon picker from catalog
description:
When user clicks '+ Add Component → Database / Cache / ...', show addon picker:
1. Fetch GET /api/v1/addon-definitions to get catalog
2. Show cards grouped by type (database, cache, queue) with icon + name + description
3. On pick, add addon card to component buffer with:
   - Component name (auto: slug of addon type, e.g. 'postgres', 'redis')
   - Addon type + chart info (from definition)
   - Exposed values form (from definition.exposed_values) — optional config like DB name, memory
   - export_schema keys shown as read-only info ('This addon exports: DATABASE_URL, POSTGRES_HOST, ...')
   - Remove button

Addon card is simpler than workload card — no source toggle, no env vars, just config values.

title: Complete cluster management flow
description: Register clusters, view cluster details, copy install command

title: Template catalog UI: browse, search, one-click deploy, params form
description: Browse/search template catalog (grid view with icons, descriptions, tags). Template detail page: services diagram, required variables form. One-click deploy: fill in params → deploy button → redirect to project view. Preview environment badge on PR-linked workloads.

title: Build logs streaming UI + build history
description: Real-time build log streaming via SSE in the workload deploy flow. Build history list with status (building/success/failed), duration, commit SHA. Click to view full build log. Failed build shows error highlighted. Retry button on failed builds.

title: Env and secrets management UI
description: Env vars tab on workload detail page. Add/edit/delete env vars inline. Show/hide secret values (masked by default). Visual indicator for auto-injected addon connection strings (read-only, labeled 'Injected by addon'). Bulk import from .env file paste.

title: Workload create page: canine-style cosmetic refresh (Option 1)
description:
Visual overhaul of /workloads/new to match the new detail page (6wq). Borrow Canine's single-page form layout: two entry modes (Git repo / Container image) via header link toggle, card-based selectors for build method (Dockerfile/Buildpacks), progressive disclosure for advanced fields. Uses existing WorkloadCreate schema — no addons, no build pipeline, no staged save. Pure cosmetic.

This is a stopgap. The real create flow (/apps/new) is the app-first create epic which converges workload-create and stack-deploy-create. This bead exists so /workloads/new doesn't look like a ghost ship meanwhile.

Reference: Canine /projects/new screenshots from 2026-04-15 playwright session.

title: Fix Toaster component import error
description: Toaster component was missing - needed use-toast.ts and toaster.tsx

title: Cloud credentials management UI
description: Settings page for managing cloud provider credentials. Form: provider selector (AWS initially), access key ID, secret access key, region, name/label. List view with masked secrets. Edit/delete. Validates via backend API on save. Route: /settings/cloud-credentials.

title: Workload logs tab: stream disconnects immediately
description: Logs tab shows 'Connecting...' then 'Log stream disconnected' with no lines. Workload is Running (test-nginx on april-3-ss). Need to verify: (a) /api/v1/workloads/{id}/logs/stream accepts ?token= query param like /events/stream does, or if it wants Authorization header only, (b) event names match what backend emits, (c) whether the Next.js proxy forwards the path correctly. Debug via browser devtools network tab + backend logs, or swap to fetch+ReadableStream if EventSource's query-token path doesn't work for this route.

title: Complete MVP authentication flow
description: Ensure login/register/logout works end-to-end with proper state management
notes:
COMPLETED: Fixed auth API endpoints to match backend (login uses form-urlencoded, register uses /user endpoint). Login and register pages now functional with proper API calls.
IN PROGRESS: Need to verify login/register flow works end-to-end.
NEXT: Test with real user credentials.

title: Cluster config wizard: first-run setup and component selection
description: First-run wizard on new cluster registration. Step 1: detect already-installed tools (show detected with checkmarks). Step 2: select components for each category (ingress, builds, gitops, metrics, logs, service mesh). Step 3: confirm + bootstrap. Show bootstrap progress in real-time. Allow reconfiguration from cluster settings page.

title: MCP config UI + AI action audit log viewer (EE)
description: Settings page: MCP server URL, API key generation, enabled tools list (toggle read/write separately). AI action log tab on audit page: filter to show only MCP-initiated actions. Pending AI approvals (for prod AI deploys in EE): approve/reject AI deploy requests. AI rate limit config: max actions per hour.

title: Workload create: build pipeline first-class (Option 2)
description:
Extend the create form with build pipeline configuration: build driver selection (local Docker vs K8s build cloud), registry credentials for push target, predeploy command, build method (Dockerfile/Buildpacks) with conditional fields. Requires extending WorkloadCreate.build_config schema on the backend.

Blocked by kubenest-ui-c0v (build logs streaming) — the build driver picker is meaningless without the pipeline behind it.

Will be superseded by the app-first create epic for new apps, but the build pipeline schema work feeds into it.

title: E2E demo UI: add missing pages for stacks, teams, RBAC, SSO login
description:
Migrated from kubenest-backend-w6a. This is UI-only work — explicitly not backend.

## Pages to build
- /login — add SSO buttons (Google, GitHub, SAML/OIDC) alongside email/password
- /stacks — Railway-style template catalog (grid of deploy cards)
- /stacks/new — create stack template (compose workloads + addons)
- /stacks/[id]/deploy — deploy from template (fill variables, pick project)
- /settings — settings landing
- /settings/teams — team management (create/add members/roles)
- /settings/rbac — roles + permissions matrix (read-only)
- /settings/sso — OIDC/SAML provider form + group mapping

## Sidebar
Add Stacks section + Settings section (Teams, Access Control, SSO).

## Rules
- Static mock data is fine; backend wiring deferred.
- Don't build real SSO / RBAC enforcement — UI only.
- Don't add backend API endpoints for this bead.
- Use existing shadcn/ui components and page patterns from /clusters.

title: Polish and error handling
description: Loading states, error pages, form validation, responsive design

title: Cluster provisioning wizard UI
description: Multi-step wizard for creating a provisioned cluster: 1) Pick provider (AWS) + select saved credential, 2) Configure: region, instance type, node count, cluster name, 3) Review + confirm, 4) Redirect to progress view. Extends existing cluster create flow with a 'Provision new cluster' option alongside 'Connect existing cluster'.

title: Complete project management flow
description: Create projects within clusters, view project details and workloads
